assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.29) when 1.28 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Regular channel clusters will be 'Force-upgraded to 1.29 automatically' with control plane upgraded first, then node pools

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.28 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support for 1.28' and mentions they can 'stay on 1.28 longer than Regular channel clusters'

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters face systematic node-level EoS enforcement — nodes on EoS versions are force-upgraded
assertions.reason: Response specifically states 'Nodes on 1.28 will be force-upgraded when EoS hits' and describes it as 'Systematic node-level EoS enforcement' with individual nodes upgraded without coordination

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response provides specific command for applying maintenance exclusion with 'no_upgrades' scope and explicitly states '30-day max' and 'Buy 30 days max to prepare'

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response consistently recommends 'Proactive upgrade (Recommended)' and includes detailed action plans with immediate, short-term, and medium-term planning phases

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'set up notifications via Cloud Logging for advance warning' in the monitoring section

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: The response does not mention the deprecation insights dashboard or API compatibility checking tools

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: The response does not mention 'snowflaking' or warn against manually freezing versions, nor does it discuss the security and reliability risks of this practice
